verses.text: Jeremiah owns God is righteous, yet asks, 'Wherefore doth the way of the wicked prosper?' God has planted them, and they grow. The old riddle troubles him. Even the faithful wrestle with the ease of the ungodly.

Jeremiah owns God is righteous, yet asks, 'Wherefore doth the way of the wicked prosper?' God has planted them, and they grow. The old riddle troubles him. Even the faithful wrestle with the ease of the ungodly.

God answers: if running with footmen has wearied him, how will he contend with horses? Even his own family deals treacherously. Harder trials lie ahead. He must learn endurance now for sterner days to come.

God answers: if running with footmen has wearied him, how will he contend with horses? Even his own family deals treacherously. Harder trials lie ahead. He must learn endurance now for sterner days to come.

God answers: if running with footmen has wearied him, how will he contend with horses? Even his own family deals treacherously. Harder trials lie ahead. He must learn endurance now for sterner days to come.
God laments, 'I have forsaken mine house,' His heritage become to Him as a hostile, speckled bird. His own people have grieved Him. The Lord's sorrow over judging His people is real and deep.

Many shepherds have ruined His vineyard; they sowed wheat but shall reap thorns. Bad leaders laid it waste. The labour spent against God's ways yields only a barren, grievous harvest.

Against His evil neighbours God will pluck up; yet if they 'learn the ways of my people,' they shall be built among them. Mercy is open even to the nations. Those who turn to the LORD find a place among His people.
